title: Authority and drift preflight
purpose: Inventory the live S2 graph modules and tests; record current schema version, seed count expectations, dataset plan, registry relationship set, Docker plugin state, M5-3/M5-4 API consumers; define live-test environment variables. Surfaces existing drift between schema registry and seed emission for explicit decision in T1.

registry_seed_drift.summary: Seed emits 11 relationship types; only 3 (INVERTS_TO, REFLECTS_AS, OPERATES_IN) are present in RELATIONSHIP_TYPE_SPECS. The other 8 are structural/graph-topology relations that pre-date the coord-driven registry.
registry_seed_drift.open_decision: T1 (Schema and relation-law convergence) must decide: expand RELATIONSHIP_TYPE_SPECS to include the seed-only structural set, OR migrate seed.rs to the smaller canonical relation set. Current tests intentionally reject ad-hoc rel types — the drift means seed emission would fail a strict registry check if such a test existed.

downstream_consumers: M5-4 mediation (Track 09) for namespace-aware graph access

dataset_import.notes: Import migration paths convert `#` -> `M`, but seed.rs preserves `#` root. Canonical decision required in T1/T3.

external_mcp.open_decision: Stays purely external/MCP-facing, or refactor to consume Rust S2 graph-service contract? TS parser documents `#` as deprecated/invalid; Rust resolves `#` intentionally — divergence risk.

docker_topology.open_decision: Whether n10s/GDS run in the engineer-facing service or in optional profiles. Doctor must report `blocked` honestly when absent.

downstream_consumer_inventory.M0_prime: Backend node facts, symbol/formulation_type provenance, OWL/SHACL/GDS readiness
downstream_consumer_inventory.M1_prime: Typed S2 relation traversal + harmonic pointer relation descriptors
downstream_consumer_inventory.M3_prime: Shared Neo4j substrate for graph/wheel surfaces; symbolic/profile provenance
downstream_consumer_inventory.M4_prime: Public S2 coordinate anchors WITHOUT leaking protected Nara/Graphiti bodies
downstream_consumer_inventory.M5_prime: Namespace-aware access: bimba | gnosis | etymology | governed protected handles
downstream_consumer_inventory.M5_3_Tauri_Theia: S2-backed graph clients, profile/pointer anchors, real local service tests
downstream_consumer_inventory.M5_4_agentic_mediation: Coordinate-native graph APIs gated by privacy/namespace law

readiness_blockers_carried_forward.summary: S2 canonical `#` root mapping — seed preserves `#`; import converts `#`->`M`; bimba-mcp TS parser deprecates `#`. T1 must reconcile.
Anuttara field naming: public aliases (symbol/formulation_type/...) vs coordinate-prefixed registry keys (c_1_symbol/c_1_formulation_type/...).
n10s/GDS packaging and GDS output persistence (derived nodes vs ephemeral overlays vs both).
Graphiti runtime boundary; what handles may appear in S2 responses before S5 review.
8 seed-emitted structural relations (GENERATES, ENTANGLES, INTERLEAVES, MANIFESTS, BEDROCK, FAMILY_CONTAINS, MOBIUS_RETURN, ANCHORED_TO) not in RELATIONSHIP_TYPE_SPECS.
External MCP parser vs Rust graph-service parser — coordinate parser/schema behavior risk.
No explicit safe path for wiping test-owned graph data without risking shared/user data.
Protected Nara/Graphiti substrate ownership before M4/4-5-0 readiness.

commands.expected: all schema-registry tests pass (relation registry, label registry, property registry, constraint plan, index plan)
all in-process tests pass; live-Neo4j tests skip if EPILOGOS_NEO4J_URI absent

commands.cmd: docker compose -f docker-compose.epi-s2.yml up -d neo4j redis
commands.expected: neo4j + redis containers running; not run during preflight
structured health report covering Neo4j, Redis, semantic cache, graph state; reports `blocked` honestly when services unreachable
